page: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-2g is an accelerator-optimized server configured with 24 vCPUs and 12 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.2 GHz. The system features x86_64 architecture, a 38 MB L3 cache, and 340.0 GB of system memory, yielding 14.17 GB of RAM per core. For acceleration, it integrates two NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs providing a combined 160 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of two NVMe SSDs totaling 804 GB.

Benchmark data indicates a clear division between CPU-bound tasks and GPU-accelerated workloads. Single-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, falls into the bottom 25% tier, while multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth register in the middle 50% tier. Conversely, the server excels in large language model inference, achieving top-tier performance in the top 10% for prompt processing and text generation across 7B and 70B parameter models.

Given its resource-dense hardware profile, this server represents a specialized investment where cost efficiency is realized through high-throughput parallel processing rather than general-purpose compute. The inclusion of dual high-capacity GPUs and local NVMe storage makes it highly suitable for deep learning, large language model inference, and GPU-accelerated data science workloads, whereas it is less optimal for single-threaded CPU tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-2g is an accelerator-optimized server designed for highly parallelized workloads. It features 24 vCPUs from an Intel Xeon processor, 340.0 GB of system memory, and 804 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The core of its performance lies in its dual NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs, which deliver a combined 160 GB of VRAM. While synthetic benchmarks show weak single-core CPU performance and average multi-core CPU capabilities, the server achieves top-tier results in large language model inference tests for both prompt processing and text generation. This hardware profile makes the server highly cost-efficient for specialized parallel computing tasks, such as deep learning and LLM execution, rather than standard general-purpose workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-2g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring 24 vCPUs, 340GB RAM, 804GB NVMe SSD storage, and two NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with 160GB total VRAM for high-end AI workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-2g features 24 vCPUs, 340GB RAM, and two NVIDIA A100 GPUs, delivering top-tier performance for LLM inference.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized platform combining dual NVIDIA A100 GPUs and high-capacity system memory for demanding machine learning and inference workloads.
bullet_points: Dual NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with 160 GB total VRAM
Intel Xeon processor with 24 vCPUs and 340 GB of system memory
804 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier benchmark performance for large language model inference
Optimized for deep learning, AI modeling, and parallel data science
